headline.pi_first_enters_through: temporal compactification (omega_k^t = 2 pi k / beta in Matsubara sum)
headline.Stefan_Boltzmann_constant_pi^2/90_origin: zeta_R(4) = pi^4/90 in temporal Matsubara sum
headline.verdict: Spatial Casimir on S^3 is pure-rational (one-projection: spatial mode-counting only).  Adding S^1_beta is a SECOND projection that introduces pi via 2 pi k / beta.  Matches Paper 34 dictionary.

spatial_T0_Casimir.textbook_reference: Bytsenko-Cognola-Elizalde-Moretti-Zerbini, 'Analytic Aspects of Quantum Fields', World Scientific 2003, sec. 4.5.  Also Dowker-Critchley PRD 13, 3224 (1976) and Ford PRD 11, 3370 (1975).

transcendental_ledger.step: spatial spectrum on unit S^3 (conformal scalar)

transcendental_ledger.transcendentals: none in form; zeta_R has algebraic values at negative odd integers via Bernoulli numbers

free_energy_high_T_beta_0.1.note: At small beta, F_thermal/F_SB_total -> 1 (high-T limit). F_SB carries pi^2 explicitly from zeta_R(4) = pi^4/90 after Matsubara sum collapses into zeta_R(-3) of the spatial modes weighted by zeta_R(4) of the temporal modes.

free_energy_mid_T_beta_1.0.note: At small beta, F_thermal/F_SB_total -> 1 (high-T limit). F_SB carries pi^2 explicitly from zeta_R(4) = pi^4/90 after Matsubara sum collapses into zeta_R(-3) of the spatial modes weighted by zeta_R(4) of the temporal modes.

free_energy_low_T_beta_10.0.note: At small beta, F_thermal/F_SB_total -> 1 (high-T limit). F_SB carries pi^2 explicitly from zeta_R(4) = pi^4/90 after Matsubara sum collapses into zeta_R(-3) of the spatial modes weighted by zeta_R(4) of the temporal modes.

matsubara_pi_origin.note: The 2 pi enters at the SPECTRUM level (omega_k^t = 2 pi k/beta). When evaluated at integer s, prefactor (2 pi/beta)^{-2s} times zeta_R(2s) = pi^{2s} * rational gives a beta^{2s} * pi^0 net, BUT the eigenvalues themselves carry 2 pi explicitly.  Casimir expansion in beta picks out terms where the net pi power is non-zero (e.g. pi^2/90 in F_SB).
